column1: core~203398;"tdf#59121 sw: add regression test for bookmark undo/redo
The bug was reported in 2013 where a sequence of Cut
column2: Undo
column3: Cut

column1: Undo
column2: Redo
column3: and Undo would restore the text but lose the bookmark.

column1: This bug has been fixed in the meantime

column1: test to prevent it from resurfacing.
This test uses the exact reproduction steps by inserting a bookmark
selecting all text

column3: and performing the Undo/Redo sequence

The bug was reported in 2013 where a sequence of Cut

column3: Cut

column1: Undo
column2: Redo
column3: and Undo would restore the text but lose the bookmark.

column1: The bug was fixed as a side effect of commit 83edee300924
(""tdf#128739 sw: don't delete bookmarks prematurely in SwUndoDelete""
2019-11-18) by Michael Stahl. tdf#128739 was primarily a crash bug
when working with fields; the fix replaced RemoveIdxFromRange() with
PaMCorrAbs() in SwUndoDelete::RedoImpl() and SwUndoInserts::UndoImpl()
which also resolved the bookmark loss in tdf#59121 without explicitly
referencing it.
This test uses the exact reproduction steps by inserting a bookmark
selecting all text
column2: cutting
column3: and performing the Undo/Redo sequence

column1: and then asserting the bookmark is properly preserved.";"KEEP";"['The new description provides additional context about the commit that fixed the bug (commit 83edee300924)

column4: and the changes made in the commit that also resolved the bookmark loss in tdf#59121 without explicitly referencing it.']";"KEEP";"The additional context provides valuable information about the history of the bug fix
column5: which can be useful for future reference and understanding of the codebase. It does not change the semantic content of the original description significantly."

column1: core~199180;"sc: syncing for SetNormalString operation";"sc: Syncing for SetNormalString operation
This reverts sorting in EnterData as we can do the sync in the
lower level with the current approach of overwriting.
We need to store the sc:ReorderParam and merge its order on change
which can be used to reverse the sorting done to the default view
to the point when the sheet view was created. Then we can reapply
the sorting to the sheet view to restore it to the last state.
As SortOrderReverser and ReorderParam are partially storing the
same data
column2: we might consider to combine them in the future.";"KEEP";"[""The old description includes 'sc:' prefix
column3: which is missing in the new description.""
column4: 'The new description provides additional context about the changes being made (reverting sorting in EnterData and storing ReorderParam to reverse sorting).']";"KEEP";"The new description provides more detailed information about the changes
column5: making it easier for others to understand the commit's purpose. The 'sc:' prefix is likely a convention or naming scheme that may not be necessary in the commit message."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1281823;"WIP: Normalize thumbnail sizing in media responses
Add a MediaFileTrait function to normalize image sizes based on
the approved thumbnail steps
column2: and use it in handlers that need

column1: to output thumbnail-oriented links. This separates the
display-oriented sizing we have for the rest of the code that
relies on sizing from the user preferences
column2: and is meant for

column1: visual sizing on the screen.";"Normalize thumbnail sizing in media responses
Add a MediaFileTrait function to normalize image sizes based on
the approved thumbnail steps
column2: and use it in handlers that need

column1: to output thumbnail-oriented links.
This commit also separates the sizing calculations to two concepts:
- Visual size of images: based on $wgImageLimits and user options.
- Physical size of thumbnails: based on $wgThumbnailSteps and follows
the strict cached sizes of the thumbnails and their urls.
Documentation was added to emphasize the difference between the two
methods. Now that the thumbnail sizing and visual sizing are actually
different
column2: we should continue to utilize the two methods where they

column1: are actually relevant.";"KEEP";"['The new description provides additional details about the separation of sizing calculations into two concepts: visual size and physical size of thumbnails
column2: with explanations for each. It also mentions the addition of documentation to emphasize the difference between the two methods.']";"KEEP";"The additional details in the new description provide a clearer understanding of the changes made and why they were necessary
column3: making it more informative for future developers."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1289374;"Special:EditWatchlist: Fix incorrect selected page count
The updateSelectedPagesListHtml() function used Array.splice() to
truncate selectedPages for display purposes. splice() mutates the
original array
column2: causing selectedPages.value.length to always equal

column1: namedLimit + 1 (6) regardless of the actual number of checked items.
This caused the 'Remove pages' button and dialog title to show an
incorrect count in languages where the translation displays the
number (e.g. Hungarian: '$1 lap eltávolítása' shows '6 lap' instead
of the real count).
Fix: Use Array.slice() with a separate displayPages variable so the
truncation is only for the HTML list display
column2: preserving the true

column1: count in selectedPages.";"Special:EditWatchlist: Fix incorrect selected page count
Array.splice() was used instead of Array.slice() to truncate the
page list for display. splice() mutates the original array
column2: causing

column1: selectedPages to lose its true count. This was visible in languages
that display the count in the button label (e.g. Hungarian).
Use a separate displayPages variable for the truncated list so that

selectedPages retains the actual number of checked items.";"KEEP";"The new description provides a more detailed explanation of why Array.splice() was causing an issue and the steps taken to fix it. It also includes a step-by-step process of how to solve the problem
column2: which is missing in the old description.";"KEEP";"The additional details provided in the new description make it more informative and easier to understand for developers who might not be familiar with the context. It also provides a clearer explanation of the solution."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1256553;"Special:Preferences: Display three options for thumbsizes
Thumbnail sizes are collapsed into three options.";"Special:Preferences: Display three options for thumbsizes
Thumbnail sizes are collapsed into three options.
The thumbnail sizes that have been removed will be rewritten
when the user nexts saves preferences or a maintenance script
is run.
The class skin-theme-clientpref-thumb-(size) added in
OutputPage will round up to the closest thumbnail size until that
happens so there should only be disruption to users who modify
preferences between this patch being deployed and the
maintenance script being run. The preferences page mirrors this logic
and can be removed once the relevant user preferences maintenance
scripts have been run.";"KEEP";"[""The new description provides additional details about the removal and rewriting of previously existing thumbnail sizes
column2: the addition of a class 'skin-theme-clientpref-thumb-(size)'
column3: and the temporary presence of the preferences page mirroring the logic until maintenance scripts are run.""]";"KEEP";"The additional details in the new description provide more context and clarity about the changes
column4: making it a better choice for users to understand the implications of the commit."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1287963;"PHPUnit 10 compatibility for parser tests via non-suite runner";"PHPUnit 10 compatibility for parser tests via non-suite runner
Instead of using the deprecated TestSuite mechanism
column2: use the

column1: `composer phpunit:config` target to generate simple 7-line
tests/phpunit/gen/ParserTest_*_Test.php files corresponding to
each tests/parser/*.txt parser test file. These test classes
use the `ParserTestFileTrait` and enumerate all the tests in
the parser test file via a standard data provider.
The `setupBeforeClass` and `tearDownAfterClass` methods are
used to create a TestFileReader and ParserTestRunner which
are shared for all the tests in a given parser test file.";"KEEP";"['The new description provides additional details about the implementation of the PHPUnit 10 compatibility for parser tests
column2: such as using a non-suite runner and generating test files with the `composer phpunit:config` command. It also mentions the use of traits and data providers
column3: and explains the purpose of `setupBeforeClass` and `tearDownAfterClass` methods.']";"KEEP";"The additional details in the new description provide a clearer understanding of the changes made
column4: which can be useful for other developers who may need to understand or maintain the codebase."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~967242;"[WIP] parser: Reduce complexity of MagicWordArray processing
Notably:
* Instead of wrapping every synonym in an individual named group
where all groups need to have the same name
column2: wrap synonyms that

column1: belong together in a single group.
* This also makes the group name unique.
* But it doesn't need to be unique anyway. PHP doesn't require that
at least not in the versions we currently use.
Unfortunately there is an external caller that uses getBaseRegex().
Luckily it doesn't do anything with the group names. It uses the
regex only for a boolean match/doesn't match check.";"parser: Reduce complexity of MagicWordArray processing
Most notably:
* Instead of wrapping every single synonym individual in a named
group that need to have identical and therefor conflicting names
wrap synonyms that belong together in a single group.
* This also makes the group name unique. No need for the ""a_""
and so on prefixes any more.
* We still need to use a single ""_"" prefix to work around the
limitation in PCRE 8.34+.
* Note that group names don't need to be unique anyway. See the
previous patch I5f113ab for an explanation.
* Also use the (?|…) syntax to make sure multiple (.*?) subpatterns
end in the expected numeric spot in the result array. The additional
test case is specifically added to prove this works. There are two
synonyms with a parameter and ""upright $1"" with a space is the
second.
There is one known external caller that uses getBaseRegex(). Luckily
it doesn't do anything with the group names. It uses the regex only
for a boolean match/doesn't match check. This still works as before.";"KEEP";"[Old] Instead of wrapping every synonym in an individual named group where all groups need to have the same name
column2: wrap synonyms that belong together in a single group.

column1: [New] Instead of wrapping every single synonym individual in a named group that need to have identical and therefor conflicting names
column2: wrap synonyms that belong together in a single group.

column1: [Old] This also makes the group name unique. But it doesn't need to be unique anyway.
[New] This also makes the group name unique. No need for the 'a_'
column3: and so on prefixes any more.

column1: [Old] We still need to use a single '_' prefix to work around the limitation in PCRE 8.34+.
[New] We still need to use a single '_' prefix to work around the limitation in PCRE 8.34+
[Old] Also use the (?|…) syntax to make sure multiple (.*?) subpatterns end in the expected numeric spot in the result array.

[New] Also use the (?|…) syntax to make sure multiple (.*?) subpatterns end in the expected numeric spot in the result array. The additional test case is specifically added to prove this works. There are two synonyms with a parameter and 'upright $1' with a space is the second.";"KEEP";"The new description provides more detailed explanations of the changes made
column2: making it easier for future developers to understand the code modifications."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1276429;"Media: Change wgThumbnailStepsRatio default from 0 to 1
This feature remains disabled by default
column2: because it is gated by

column1: $wgThumbnailSteps
column2: which remains null by default.

column1: However
column2: when people enable the feature
column3: for example to locally test

column1: something in a way that represents WMF prod
column2: it should be as easy as

column1: setting `$wgThumbnailStepsRatio = [ ... ];` and not require

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1287016;"ParserOptions: absoluteURLs should default to false
This was a regression introduced in
The default value for absoluteURLs (from ParserOutput::getText()
historically) has always been false. When the text options
were turned into ParserOptions this was inadvertently flipped.
Follows-Up: I4184dc74fdd107031b821039e113126c5f1beb03";"ParserOptions: absoluteURLs should default to false
The default value for absoluteURLs (from ParserOutput::getText()
historically) has always been false. When the text options
were turned into ParserOptions this was inadvertently flipped.
This was a regression introduced in

column2: but latent until

column1: Icf7172be6d718362192ca8b7288ef8ce4d4af6b9 added
ParserOutputAccess::postprocess() which created the
getText options"" $textOptions from the ParserOptions
picking up the (incorrect) default value. Further
column2: the

column1: postprocessing cache was only enabled in production in
a staggered fashion through early 2026.
Due to the way ParserOptions defaults work
column2: changing the default

column1: value here won't invalidate any parser cache entries. Previously
cached contents with the old default (absoluteURLs=true) will
continue to match requests made with the new default
(absoluteURLs=false). It will take a parser cache expiration
time for all cache contents to be updated. That should be
okay since the absolute URLs are still valid for production
wikis
column2: just not strictly necessary (and there was a small

column1: performance penalty for doing the url conversion).
Follows-Up: I4184dc74fdd107031b821039e113126c5f1beb03";"KEEP";"[""The new description provides additional context about the regression
column2: the introduction of ParserOutputAccess::postprocess()
column3: and the staggered enabling of the postprocessing cache in production. It also explains why changing the default value won't invalidate any parser cache entries.""]";"KEEP";"The additional context provided in the new description helps to better understand the issue
column4: its impact
column5: and the reasoning behind the fix. This makes it more informative and useful for future reference."

column1: mediawiki%2Fcore~1290772;"Add a postproc parseroption for edit link behaviour
VisualEditor uses the onSkinEditSectionLinks hook to provide the correct
link(s) to edit the page. The behaviour is as follows
column2: depending on wiki

column1: setup and user preferences:
- if both links should be displayed
column2: both links are displayed

column1: - if a single link should be displayed
column2: it is displayed as ""edit"" if VE

column1: should be used
column2: or as ""edit source"" otherwise.

column1: This behaviour is handled during the HandleSectionEditLink
post-processing passes; but it's not splitting the cache depending on
what gets rendered. In this patch
column2: we introduce ""visibleLinks"" as a

column1: cache-varying ParserOption to handle this variation.
The behaviour depends on particular on wgVisualEditorUseSingleEditTab
which enables the re-use of the editor and section link for some wikis.
This setting is not set to true everywhere
column2: and is marked in our config

column1: as ""Deploying slowly with community advanced notice"". To avoid
accidentally bypassing this setting
column2: the the default value for the new

column1: ParserOption is ""both""
column2: which displays both links as needed.

column1: Ideally
column2: this should go on the same train as

column1: Ife470688200d19f343aba7d2826855078eef2f68 to fix the issue as
soon as possible. Otherwise
column2: the cache key won't be updated - this

column1: should not be an issue with regards to cache misses
column2: but the default

column1: cache entry may not be the right one depending on the current wiki
configuration.";"Make ParserOptions available to Skin
VisualEditor uses the onSkinEditSectionLinks hook to provide the correct
link(s) to the page. This is called during the HandleSectionEditLink
post-processing passes
column2: but it's not splitting the cache depending on

column1: what gets rendered.
To be able to split the cache during this step
column2: we make the

column1: ParserOptions available to the skin before we start the post-processing
pass.";"KEEP";"[""The new description focuses on making ParserOptions available to Skins
column2: while the old one introduces a new cache-varying ParserOption 'visibleLinks' to handle variation in link behavior. The old description also provides more context about the current behavior and the reason for introducing the new ParserOption.""]";"KEEP";"The new description is more concise and focuses on the main change
column3: which is making ParserOptions available to Skins. However
column4: the old description provides important context about the current behavior and the reason for the change
column5: which can be useful for understanding the full scope of the commit."
